news.title: Noida International Airport to begin commercial operations from June 15
news.para: Noida International Airport (NIA) in Jewar will commence commercial flight operations on June 15, 2026, with IndiGo operating the inaugural flight. Akasa Air and Air India Express are slated to follow soon after. The airport, developed by YIAPL, will initially handle 1.2 crore passengers annually, with plans for significant expansion.

news.title: Telangana HC pulls up HYDRAA over illegal construction in Salkam Cheruvu FTL area
news.para: The Telangana High Court has sharply questioned HYDRAA's failure to act against the Barrister Fatima Owaisi Educational Campus. The institution is allegedly built in the Full Tank Level area of Salkam Cheruvu without proper approvals. The court ordered a detailed inquiry into officials who neglected their duties. Students' safety was also a concern raised by the judge.

news.title: Goa's town planning department proposes to tag 28.4 lakh sq meter as ‘non-developable’
news.para: Goa's Town and Country Planning department is proposing to designate vast land tracts as non-developable. This move aims to protect significant natural areas. Three major parcels in Bardez and Pernem, covering over 28 lakh square meters, are included. These proposals are now open for public objections. This marks a shift from previous land use policies.

news.title: House prices in UK rise in April despite Iran war headwinds: Nationwide
news.para: UK house prices have surprised economists by rising for the fourth consecutive month in April. This upward trend continues despite global uncertainties and a dip in consumer confidence. Strong household finances and income growth are supporting the housing market. Nationwide Building Society reported a 0.4% increase in April, with prices now 3.0% higher year-on-year.

news.title: The Indian Hotels Company's amã Stays & Trails enters Andaman & Nicobar Islands
news.para: The Indian Hotels Company (IHCL) has expanded its homestay portfolio to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands with a 10-bungalow cluster in Port Blair. Separately, the company concluded a ₹222-crore investment to acquire a majority stake in Brij Hospital.

news.title: Kolhapur civic body’s survey finds 204 illegal constructions near Tawde hotel, 88 in contempt of court
news.para: A Kolhapur Municipal Corporation survey revealed 204 illegal constructions on 158 acres of civic land near Tawde hotel, with 88 violating court orders. Thirty-three structures are within the Panchaganga river's flood line, posing environmental and safety risks. The survey followed a Supreme Court ruling confirming KMC's ownership of the land.

news.title: EFC (I) to raise ₹160 crore via rights issue at ₹150 per share
news.para: Managed workspace operator EFC (I) Limited has finalised terms for a rights issue of over 1.06 crore equity shares at ₹150 apiece, and separately incorporated a wholly-owned subsidiary to hold commercial property in Pune's Shivaji Nagar.

news.title: Allocate 2BHK houses by May-end: Telangana housing minister
news.para: Housing Minister Ponguleti Srinivas Reddy has ordered officials to allocate double-bedroom houses by May's end, prioritizing those nearing completion. Partially built homes will receive up to ₹5 lakh aid, with beneficiaries allowed to finish construction if contractors refuse. Ineligible occupants will be removed, ensuring poverty remains the key eligibility factor.

news.title: Concorde leases 1.4 lakh sq ft space to BHIVE in Bengaluru; TEC adds 90,000 sq ft in Mumbai & Pune
news.para: Concorde has leased out 1.4 lakh sq ft to BHIVE Workspaces in Bengaluru, creating room for more than 3,000 professionals. On the other hand, The Executive Centre is gearing up for growth, with plans to unveil new locations in Mumbai and Pune, expanding its co-working offerings significantly.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh to upgrade 100 more small towns under phase-II of aspirational towns scheme
news.para: Uttar Pradesh government is launching the second phase of its aspirational towns programme. This initiative will upgrade 100 more backward urban local bodies. The scheme focuses on improving basic necessities and civic amenities. It will run until March 31, 2028. Key areas include urban and social infrastructure, ULB buildings, water bodies, and parks.

news.title: Hubballi-Dharwad civic body extends 5% property tax rebate till May 31
news.para: The Hubballi-Dharwad Municipal Corporation has extended the 5% property tax concession period for the 2026-27 financial year until May 31. This decision follows a 12-day delay in tax collection due to technical issues and a series of festivals in April. The extension was supported by both the house leader and opposition leader.

news.title: Prayagraj development body launches OTS to help defaulters clear dues
news.para: Prayagraj Development Authority offers relief to hundreds of allottees with overdue payments. A new One-Time Settlement scheme allows defaulters to clear outstanding dues. Penal interest will be waived. The remaining principal can be paid in six to seven installments. This initiative aims to resolve long-pending financial issues for property owners.

news.title: Nashik civic body collects ₹76 crore property tax in April 2026
news.para: Nashik Municipal Corporation's property tax collection surged by 61.71% to Rs76.19 crore in April, driven by an 8% early-payment rebate. Over 1.55 lakh property owners availed the scheme, boosting confidence in achieving the Rs285 crore annual target. The civic body is also focusing on identifying unassessed properties to sustain revenue growth.

news.title: Gujarat HC orders aeronautical survey before demolition near SVPI airport
news.para: The Gujarat High Court has ordered the Airports Authority of India to conduct an aeronautical survey of buildings after developers challenged demolition notices. The court found the authorities' insistence on demolition before a survey to be unjustified and arbitrary, quashing the notices while allowing for potential removal post-survey.

news.title: Allahabad HC upholds land acquisition for Noida airport expansion, stresses rehabilitation measures
news.para: The Allahabad High Court has green-lighted the land acquisition necessary for expanding the Noida International Airport. The court dismissed objections from local landowners, affirming that the acquisition adhered to the 2013 land law. Importantly, it mandated comprehensive rehabilitation for existing residential land occupants before any possession occurs, paving the way for the airport's ambitious growth plans.

news.title: Chennai civic body enables QR code to fast-track property tax payments
news.para: Greater Chennai Corporation has introduced QR code payments for property tax to expedite collections following poll-related staff deployment delays. The civic body aims to collect the remaining ₹400 crore of its ₹900 crore annual revenue target, with over 14 lakh assessees. This initiative follows a successful early payment incentive scheme last year.

news.title: Karnataka government approves Greater Bengaluru's ₹18,000 crore suburban township
news.para: Karnataka government has approved the Rs 18,000-crore Greater Bengaluru integrated suburban township project, a 7,481-acre 'work-live-play' initiative spanning Bengaluru South district. The state will guarantee Rs 7,500 crore from Hudco, with the remaining funds sourced from BDA and CA site monetization. Land acquisition compensation is set at over Rs 2 crore per acre or equivalent developed land.

news.title: Government replaces building code with a new standard, retains fire safety provisions as 'advisory'
news.para: Government has replaced the National Building Code with National Building Construction Standards. These new standards are now voluntary guidelines. A significant change is in fire and life safety rules. Previously mandatory, these provisions are now advisory. The height threshold for these rules has also increased. This shift aims for more flexibility and innovation in construction.

news.title: Prayagraj: PDA allows multiple family members to buy flats under new housing norms
news.para: The Prayagraj Development Authority has introduced new rules allowing multiple family members to purchase separate homes. This policy aims to boost housing sales and clear unsold properties. The authority will also partner with brokers to renovate and sell vacant flats. These changes are expected to make housing more accessible and affordable for buyers in Prayagraj.

news.title: MHADA drops flat prices in Vikhroli to 7.5% owing to poor public response
news.para: Mhada has reduced prices by 7.5% for 1,221 flats in Vikhroli, effectively cutting rates by Rs 10 lakh per unit. This move comes after poor public response to its lotteries, as once affordable flats now match market prices. Buyers are opting for private builders offering better quality and amenities.
